steps.title: Complete subject and verb
steps.content: The sentence needs 'Ann Quinby of Kentucky played an important role.' The location completes the subject, and 'played' is the main verb.

steps.title: Reject punctuation breaks
steps.content: Choice A creates the fragment 'Played an important role.' Choice B uses a colon between the subject and verb. Choice C uses a semicolon after a phrase that is not an independent clause. The remaining choice keeps the subject and verb together without punctuation. The answer is D.
